L. 95–452October 1, 2014“(1)in the case of section 411—“(A)the date of the first nomination by the President of an individual to serve as the Director of the National Reconnaissance Office that occurs on or after ; orOctober 1, 2014“(B)the date of the cessation of the performance of the duties of the Director of the National Reconnaissance Office by the individual performing such duties on ; andOctober 1, 2014“(2)in the case of section 412—“(A)the date of the first nomination by the President of an individual to serve as the Inspector General of the National Reconnaissance Office that occurs on or after ; orOctober 1, 2014“(B)the date of the cessation of the performance of the duties of the Inspector General of the National Reconnaissance Office by the individual performing such duties on .October 1, 2014“(b).—Exception for Initial NominationsNotwithstanding paragraph (1)(A) or (2)(A) of subsection (a), an individual serving as the Director of the National Reconnaissance Office or the Inspector General of the National Reconnaissance Office on the date that the President first nominates an individual for such position on or after , may continue to perform in that position after such date of nomination and until the individual appointed to the position, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, assumes the duties of the position.October 1, 2014“(c).—Incumbent Inspector GeneralThe individual serving as Inspector General of the National Reconnaissance Office on the date of the enactment of this Act [] shall be eligible to be appointed by the President to a new term of service under , United States Code, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.”July 7, 2014section 403 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: There is a Director of the National Reconnaissance Office.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: b Appointment
children.text: The Director of the National Reconnaissance Office shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.

children.heading: Functions and duties
children.label: c Functions and duties
children.text: The Director of the National Reconnaissance Office shall be the head of the National Reconnaissance Office and shall discharge such functions and duties as are provided by this chapter or otherwise by law or executive order.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the National Reconnaissance Office an advisory board (in this section referred to as the “Board”).

children.label: A
children.text: study matters relating to the mission of the National Reconnaissance Office, including with respect to promoting innovation, competition, and resilience in space, overhead reconnaissance, acquisition, and other matters; and

children.label: B
children.text: advise and report directly to the Director with respect to such matters.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Board shall be composed of up to 8 members appointed by the Director, in consultation with the Director of National Intelligence and the Secretary of Defense, from among individuals with demonstrated academic, government, business, or other expertise relevant to the mission and functions of the National Reconnaissance Office, and who do not present any actual or potential conflict of interest.

children.heading: Membership structure
children.label: ii Membership structure
children.text: The Director shall ensure that no more than 2 concurrently serving members of the Board qualify for membership on the Board based predominantly on a single qualification set forth under clause (i).

children.heading: Notification
children.label: iii Notification
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the Director appoints a member to the Board, the Director shall notify the congressional intelligence committees and the congressional defense committees (as defined in ) of such appointment.section 101(a) of title 10

children.heading: Terms
children.label: B Terms
children.text: Each member shall be appointed for a term of 2 years. Except as provided by subparagraph (C), a member may not serve more than three terms.

children.heading: Vacancy
children.label: C Vacancy
children.text: Any member appointed to fill a vacancy occurring before the expiration of the term for which the member’s predecessor was appointed shall be appointed only for the remainder of that term. A member may serve after the expiration of that member’s term until a successor has taken office.

children.heading: Chair
children.label: D Chair
children.text: The Board shall have a Chair, who shall be appointed by the Director from among the members.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: E Travel expenses
children.text: Each member shall receive travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, in accordance with applicable provisions under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5.

children.heading: Executive secretary
children.label: F Executive secretary
children.text: The Director may appoint an executive secretary, who shall be an employee of the National Reconnaissance Office, to support the Board.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 4 Meetings
children.text: The Board shall meet not less than quarterly, but may meet more frequently at the call of the Director.

children.label: A
children.text: Mandatory processes for identifying potential conflicts of interest, including the submission of initial and periodic financial disclosures by Board members.

children.label: B
children.text: The vetting of potential conflicts of interest by the designated agency ethics official, except that no individual waiver may be granted for a conflict of interest identified with respect to the Chair of the Board.

children.label: C
children.text: The establishment of a process and associated protections for any whistleblower alleging a violation of applicable conflict of interest law, Federal contracting law, or other provision of law.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 6 Reports
children.text: Not later than March 31 of each year, the Board shall submit to the Director and to the congressional intelligence committees a report on the activities and significant findings of the Board during the preceding year.

children.heading: Nonapplicability of certain requirements
children.label: 7 Nonapplicability of certain requirements
children.text: The Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) shall not apply to the Board.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 8 Termination
children.text: The Board shall terminate on .August 31, 2027
